long_description: The radar processor computer fails in flight, leading to loss of radar modes, unreliable tracks, or tactical display corruption.
pilot_actions: Recycle radar mode if permitted, disregard suspect radar tracks, transition to visual or alternate sensor tactics, and continue only if mission rules allow.

long_description: Radar processor or associated radar electronics overheat in flight, causing mode dropout, intermittent resets, or forced radar shutdown.
pilot_actions: Reduce radar duty cycle, select less demanding modes if possible, monitor temperature warnings, and recover if tactical awareness depends on radar availability.

long_description: Radar tracks freeze or stop updating in flight despite continued display presence, creating false target confidence and stale intercept cues.
pilot_actions: Treat non-updating tracks as invalid, cross-check with other sensors, and avoid weapon employment based solely on frozen radar data.

long_description: Radar timing reference or synchronization degrades in flight, causing ranging errors, unstable mode behavior, or target-processing faults.
pilot_actions: Use simpler radar modes if available, distrust fine ranging solutions, and recover if radar performance is required for safe mission continuation.

long_description: RF chain performance degrades in flight, reducing radar detection range, track stability, or look-up/look-down effectiveness.
pilot_actions: Expect shorter detection range, close tactical spacing conservatively, and transition to alternate sensors if radar utility falls below mission need.
